title: tibouchina citrina
intro: Tibouchina citrina, also known as the 'Gold Coin' or 'Lindenia', is a flowering plant native to South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The leaves and flowers of the plant have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and respiratory infections, with its health benefits also including antioxidant, antiseptic, and anti-histamine properties. Tibouchina citrina can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a versatile plant for various herbal preparations.
